title: cotyledon edulis var. attenuata
intro: Cotyledon edulis var. attenuata, also known as the dragon's blood succulent, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin issues. The plant's extracts have been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Cotyledon edulis var. attenuata include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally, depending on the specific preparation and intended use.
